title: Whipped Filipino Beef Bistek
description: A sweet, silky, quick-fix Filipino beef dish featuring speck, mushimono-steam, no-onion, no-garlic.
instructions: Whisk soy sauce, calamansi juice, brown sugar, fish sauce, sesame oil, black pepper, and water until sugar dissolves.
Toss the sliced beef into the bowl, coat evenly, cover and refrigerate for 15 minutes.
Heat a skillet over medium-high heat and add diced speck, cooking until crisp and fat renders.
Remove excess speck fat, then add the marinated beef, searing 2 minutes per side until browned.
Sprinkle cornstarch over the beef, stir to coat, and cook for an additional 30 seconds to thicken the sauce.
Transfer the beef to a heatproof dish, pour remaining sauce over it, and cover tightly with foil.
Steam the beef in a steamer or microwave for 2 minutes, until fully cooked.
Remove the foil, fluff the beef with a fork, and whisk briefly to achieve a silky texture.
Serve the whipped beef over steamed rice or noodles.
Enjoy this sweet, silky Filipino beef bistek.
